expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (position_variables, (DLCPosV1 & key).fetch1_dataframe, ClusterlessDecodingSelection, PositionGroup).
Identifies variable-name mismatch (NOT empty DLC output) as root cause
expectations.evidence: Opening: 'DLC's output is fine. DLCPosV1.fetch1_dataframe() already emits a column literally named position_x ... Overriding it with [head_position_x, head_position_y] points at columns that do not exist'.

expectations.text: Shows (DLCPosV1 & key).fetch1_dataframe().columns as the concrete diagnostic
expectations.evidence: Concrete diagnostic section: `cols = list((DLCPosV1 & key).fetch1_dataframe().columns)` with expected output.

expectations.text: Recognizes that the default position_variables=['position_x', 'position_y'] matches DLC
expectations.evidence: 'PositionGroup.create_group's default position_variables=[position_x, position_y] matches both upstream sources without modification'.

expectations.text: Names _upsample as the line that raises KeyError when position_variables references columns that don't exist
expectations.evidence: Quotes the exact `_upsample` code (`for column in position_variable_names: ... position_df[column].isna()`) and explains 'pandas raises KeyError: head_position_x, and you see it bubble out of _upsample / fetch_position_info'.

expectations.text: Does not recommend re-running DLC populate as the fix
expectations.evidence: What-NOT-to-do: 'Don't re-run DLC populate. The DLC outputs already have the columns the decoder expects'.
